text: T-Mobile is terrible. You’re bill will never be the same and will definitely never go lower it’s always some bullcrao and talking to them is like talking to a wall

I don’t understand why there’s no oversight for companies like this.
Literally thousands of poor reviews, BBB,this platform,Google etc.all w/same  theme,billing issues,generally poor connectivity & poor customer service &  , God forbid you want out…you’re punished w/continued auto debit or bills.
Reputable companies don’t try to make you stay.

Bad service. Calls randomly end on my Galaxy S24 ultra and previously owned phones (Galaxy A54, One Plus Nord N 30). I've called Samsung and T Mobile too many times do the same troubleshooting steps over and over but still didn't fix the issue. It can't be the phone because it has happened on more than one of my devices

I am deeply disappointed with the level of customer service I received from T-Mobile. During my recent interaction, the representatives I spoke with were shockingly unknowledgeable and unprofessional. I called with a relatively simple issue, but instead of providing clear answers, the rep seemed confused and had no idea how to address my concerns.
When I asked for clarification, I was met with vague responses and several " i cant do that." It became clear that this representative lacked even basic knowledge about the services and products T-Mobile offers. To make matters worse, their attitude was indifferent and dismissive, making me feel like my issue wasn’t important to them at all.
After spending over 45 minutes on the phone without a resolution, I was left frustrated and no closer to solving my problem. It’s shocking that a company as large as T-Mobile would allow such untrained and unprofessional representatives to interact with customers. This experience has seriously made me reconsider continuing with their service.
T-Mobile, if you want to keep your customers, you need to invest in better training and hire people who actually care about providing help. This experience was unacceptable.
The best telecommunication company in the US!

A tmobile store "gave" me a "free" phone when I renewed a contract.  The tmobile store illegally signed me up for a new line, which is why  the phone was free.  When I cancelled the illegally added line, I had to pay $500 for the phone.  Now the phone can not even be unlocked even though it is paid for and my account is in good standing (for 14 years).  Time to make a FTC complaint and get a new carrier. I have spent over 10 hours talking to support and supervisor's in at least 10 different calls, the latest (and last) being today.  They are truly incompetent.

Used my TMobile iphone on a densely populated street in a U.S. city called Chicago. A metropolitan area of 8,984,000. At 6:29 pm CDT on 8/26/2024, received the following text from TMobile customer service:

"We hope that you are enjoying your plan benefits! Your usage has been roaming off our network, which violates our terms and conditions. If this line ( ) continues to roam extensively over the next month, your line will be canceled."

Called TMobile customer service to ensure it wasn't spam. It wasn't. I was advised to get a Tmobile coverage map to avoid this heinous error in the future. Yes, I am to memorize the Tmobile coverage map at all times, street-by-street, in the center of the 3rd largest city in the USA. Assumed that, because TMobile has 77 stores in a city of this size,this carrier would have full coverage. Bad customer that I am, I was wrong. I learned that I need to memorize the TMobile coverage map in Chicago. And most everywhere that I use the phone. I have learned the hard way, after 18 months of timely payments totaling $1566, I learned what an awful customer I am. One more incident like this and TMobile will terminate my line. I only wish I could attach a copy of the above text from TMobile to share with the world. If TMobile wonders the identity of the evil customer who posted this review, then I suggest that they check texts they sent at 6:29 pm CDT on Monday, August 26, 2024. Oh, and I then learnt that the "roaming zone" in question was within TMobile's prime 5G area.

I was with T-mobile for very long time. Even when they whitelisted my phone for being too old. When I got tired of spending money on a phone that only allowed me to use it like an old dial phone, I decided to switch to another carrier. Here's a tip, don't tell them you are switching until you have switched, otherwise they will turn off your phone, where you got all of your access codes to your T-mobile account, apple account, work accounts, etc., and then they will cancel your account before you can get your correct account number, which by the way cannot be found in your account. After nearly 10 years with them they rewarded me with aggravation and contempt. Let the phone subscriber beware!! T-Mobile sucks!!!

DO NOT SIGN UP FOR T MOBILE!  Bait and Switch.  The hours and hours on horrible customer service to get no where.  Promised service at $35 per line.  Credit for trade in.  Now $55 per line, they have my phone and nothing I can do.  Will not honor $35 even with recorded call stating $35.
STAY FAR AWAY!
Why customers got to pay extra 5 dollars to make. Payment in store when store is empty and nothing going on and the coverage sucks half time. Makes no sense

Brandon from the staples Store T-Mobile was very knowledgeable, and very helpful. He programmed my phone, because I had no idea what I was doing. Very good customer service and I will refer my friends to this store. Thanks Brandon.

The t-mobile agents are not honest they told me that the internet is 50 dollars a month but when i received it and activated the device it was 60 dollars a month and the phone services when i switched to t-mobile was awful i canceled it the same day i got it and they didnt refund me the full money i paid for they only refunded 16 dollars of the amount 43 dollars i paid for .. they were terrible in every way i will never choose t-mobile again.

I left T-Mobile May 31st this year for a cheaper option. Two weeks later they billed me for the upcoming month. I called 9 time since then and still have not received a refund for their billing error. Suffice it to say, I will NEVER return to T-Mobile!

The wors experience ever for a prepaid sim card. 1 They do not accept European credit cards (they just do not accept them, why? Idk). 2. Once your number got deactivated you are no longer in a position to pay for another month... In order to reactivate it, you need to log in into your T-mobile account, but it requires... receiving a text. Once the number is deactivated, you cannot receive any texts. 

Worst experience ever. DON'T NOT GET T-MOBILE!!!

Everything was going fine with t mobile until you suggested.Changing my account to save money.  Once they did this, They broke my account into parts that I didn't even know they were billing.  Now they are asking me to pay back charges that we're in a account I wasn't even billed for.  DO NOT USE TMOBILE!
A representative told me to oay $150 and my services turned only to be told that I needed an extra $230. It's so low to lie to a customer to get them off the phone.

I would not recommend them to anyone worst company and obviously liars who are selling defective Iphone 15 to customers and refuse to send a replacement when the problem with the phones are not caused by the customers. My phone is not even a year and I have called them 6 times regarding the situation and they refused to replace because I do not have insurance. Why should I when the problem us with the product you sold. I cannot wait to get another company to buy out my remaining contract from them. They have gone down hill money and greed is what's driving them now. They no longer value their customers. Do not switch to them.

Went to T-Mobile and Jose Z help me with my phone problem. I couldn’t update some apps. He spent less than a hour to update my phone change my screen cove. He is very professional and easy to talk to. I would recommend anyone that uses T-Mobile to go visit Jose Z at the quarry. He’s awesome!

T-Mobile bad news don’t buy - I phone 15 - from T- Mobile store there no good you will get a problem with the camera and- t mobile will do nothing and Apple wants to charge you $1,200.00 to fix camera rip off t mobile is bad news my phone is new yet and have insurance on phone and to replace my phone they want a lot...

I switched to T-Mobile from AT&T in July 2024 for my iPhone only. I chose Go5G Next. The same plan is designed for Yearly upgrades. This left my Android phone on my family's AT&T.

This means I have to get an iPhone 16 Pro Max in September 2024 to start my iPhone Yearly Upgrade. It would be an iPhone upgrade from my iPhone 14 Pro Max.

As far as the cell service and internet service, they are good.  It's the supports they have in place that fail at both the stores and tech support.  When I added lines, the tech at the store told me one thing and it ended up being the opposite causing me money, stress, anxiety and frustration.  Eventually it was corrected, but geez.  He also never told me about the requirement to register my liquid screen for my phone for the warranty coverage.  He is no longer at that store.  I decided to get T-Mobile Inet, tried to order the service online, and had issues, so I went to the store and the tech told me that he could do it for me.  He cancelled my order and setup up my equipment.  However, he did not tell me by doing this my $200 rebate went to $100, which I just found out while returning to the store.  He instructed me to call tech support for any additional items needed for the Inet.  Well, the mesh needed it. I was also told that the 2.4g and 5g could be separated and certain devices will not work on the 5g.  I was on the phone with tech support and after 2+ hours, they could not figure out how to do that.  Then I went in for a warranty and was texted and emailed the product was there and they could not find it.  The tech handed it to me and said set it up like before.  I told him I did not set it up the store did.  There were issues with his capability to do that.  Then when I asked both of them about the internet and the 2.4g vs 5 it was deer with the headlights look and neither of them could help, nor did they offer to.  So back to the drawing board and figuring it out.  They are car salesmen and will tell you what they need to for the order and that is very disappointing.  At least with Spectrum they come to you and set things up.  Overpriced Inet, but they help.  I am frustrated as I can link all of my home devices to this Inet because of this issue which in turn impacts my home security.  The product the provide is worthless if the customers can't use it and/or don't understand how.  Then lets not forget the issue with the phone service (land line) if you want it.  That is a 3rd party and not explained until after you get the product.  Again, it's the techs who are not properly trained to understand and help the customer understand.  BTW, the store was dead and still no offer to help.  The playlist provided by corporate was vulgar.  I'm done now.  Get it together T-Mobile.

The majority of the customer care specialists have no clue what they are doing.  They read a script.   I had to call multiple times this week trying to resolve and issue with a phone and it was HORRIBLE. I wasted hours on the phone - one rep was very helpful but the info she gave was incorrect - and led to more calls and trips to the store - then I escalated to a supervisor - only to find out after 90 minutes on the phone with him - the info he gave me wasn't accurate because he is in a different time zone - my question was - how do I know that?  Shouldn't he know that?   So they told me they were trying just to set the expectations - it ended with my reply of - no your not setting the expectations - you are telling me that once again T-Mobile is failing and not fulfilling their obligations for the 2nd time this week.   I've been with Sprint/T-mobile for 21 years and am seriously considering a change.

I sent this via email to the CEO (update:  Mike replied and indicated that he appreciated my email.  He will reward those who helped me.  He was very concerned about the overall low rating of t mobile on Trustpilot.  He will look into this).

Mike,

This is written to show my deep appreciation for the three amazing employees who made my day, my week, and perhaps my year.  I am not exaggerating.  I was a customer of your competition and I went into your store because I wanted to get a good deal on mobile phone service.  Your team took the time to get me a great deal, patiently educated me on how to move from my current provider over to T Mobile.  The process was not quick and in fact it took over two hours to complete the process of moving two phones to the T Mobile system and eventually moving all of my data from my old phones to the new iPhone 15 models.  

The district manager was present and offered me a great deal on internet and a land line. He even told me that the price for service would never change.  Incredible.  

Please extend a thank you to this great team of employees.  They are Anthony, Lorenni, and Yassine.  They work at the store in Exton, PA at the Main Street shopping center.  

I find that the best way to keep great employees is to show your appreciation.  As the leader, you set the example.  Do the right thing. 

Thank you for taking the time to read this.  

Sincerely,

Jeff Davidson 
Satisfied customer since today... Keep it that way!




Jeffrey A. Davidson, Ph.D.
CEO, Alpha Kappa Statistics, LLC 
267.400.0219  |

I was a loyal customer for 18 years---18 YEARS. When I moved to my new apartment 2 months ago I could not get any reception for my phone or home internet. I contacted T-Mobile numerous times and was told since there was a hospital between me and the tower, that I would not be able to get reception. So I reluctantly had to leave T-Mobile. Then their tune changed. I kept getting billed for things, claiming I didn't return some type of hardware I supposedly got in 2015. I would call and get a different story from the representative each time The last straw was them sending me a collection notice for a bill that they had never sent me for that supposed hardware. The actual T-Mobile bill didn't show up for 4 days after the collection notice which is just wrong. I should have left T-Mobile years ago.

I have been a customer of Tmobile since they took over Sprint and about 10 years with Sprint.  My bill went up $20 and they told me it was because I paid my phones off.  So had I kept paying for phones it would have been less expensive. I was then told they would give me a credit for being a loyal customer for so many years and I asked if that was permanent.  They told me they would check.  I called today and the lady I spoke with was rude and condescending and said you arent going to get credit for something you arent paying for.  I told her cancel my service and hung up.  She called back and I said I have nothing left to say to you she said i cant cancel your service if you hang up.  I said well you were extremely rude have someone else call me because I dont want to talk to you. no one has called back yet.

I read some of the reviews and have never experienced anything like the complaints. T-Mobile gives people extensions, not only that they offer to split the late bill in two. It is not their fault people don't know how to budget. I have always had a great customer service experience and I have had gone T-Mobile everything even down to the bank account. I love T-Mobile because they have the best customer service people ever.

My family has had T-mobile for six years and the past couple of months have really made me regret choosing them as my cell phone provider. My son’s phone was not letting him make or receive calls or text with the data service only with Wi-Fi. My husband has been to a T-mobile store and has talked to several T-mobile representatives on the phone to rectify this issue. Long story short… the phone is still not fixed but we still get a full bill every month for four phones and can only use three. If that’s not stealing I don’t know what is. Your job is to provide customers with service and satisfaction we have received neither for two months. As a parent it is vital for me to be able to contact and communicate with my child through our cell phones. This company has stressed my entire family out behind this one issue that an entire company can’t seem to resolve. I will wholeheartedly be in the market for a new and more trustworthy cell phone carrier immediately.

Absolutely the worst wireless company in the country! 
Aside from dropped calls almost daily and messages not going through the customer service is atrocious. 
I have never dealt with such stupidity and ignorance and I was with Bank of America...that's saying something! 
They will literally lock your phone even after you've paid your bill forcing to pay again.
The lies and games are truly unbelievable.
It's no wonder out of every single carrier in the country they were rated the absolute worst...on a scale of 1-100 they scored a 51.
I'm not surprised but so grateful to be gone and to never have to deal with their terrible "lack of customer service" agents ever again!

I give T mobile zero stars you're on hold for over three hours. I've proof on my phone. After 55 minutes was hung up on. I don't know what happened to tmobile.  The manager, Leo   very rude disrespectful to woman, he belittle me the entire call. I don't know why Tmobile is hiring unprofessional people that are 

just outrageous and customer service representative are unprofessional not helpful at  all very incompetent when it comes to resolving issues. I'm going to find another provider.  I , dont recommend Tmobile poor service and they steal your money and charge you for services you don't have check your bill. 
Tmobile should be ashamed of themselves. ::;((((((((( Leo manager should be terminated immediately check phone conversation. It made me cry how rude a man can be to a female customer:::(((((((((

Where to get started? Buckle up for the complete crap show that is t-mobile kids bc its a doozy. 

To start.. i have a samsung galaxy s22Ultra i wanted to keep my phone when i changed servies to t mobile. The man at the store told me i had to BUY a phone from them in order to get the sim card for tmobile. My daughter also got a phone. We were told her phone was only 30 and mine was 7 and that was it we just had to pay the start up. WELL LET ME TELL YOU thats where the lies began. I was NOT informed id be paying MONTHLY on the phones. Two phones one that I didnt even NEED bc i had my own phone. Their customer service told me i didnt need to buy a phone for the sim card.. but dude in store said i did. 
    They told me my bill would be 80 a month for 2 lines of UNLIMITED 5G NEVER SLOWS DOWN SERVICE.... my bill is about 98 a month and more often than not random charges get put on my bill. 
    "My speeds will never slow down no matter how much data I use" bold face lie. My data slows down and i have to CALL customer service for them to reset it. They told me to call every month to have it done if it slows down. Why should I have to?!?! I was blatantly lied to by this crook of a company on so many aspects of this entire situation. 
    The service is trash. My phone takes forever to load things even on their "5G" and half the time my phone goes to 4GLTE WHICH IS SLOWER THAN WHAT I PAY FOR!! I have to use my wifi at home lately bc service is so slow things wont load its ridiculous.  Do not waste your money on this company. Shady business!

This is a PSA for any T-mobile users out there. Watch yourself, cause T-mobile is out here stealing peoples money. I was with them for 2 long years. First, it started off with them telling me they could lower my bill, the price they quoted me was not the price I saw when I got my bills every month, service was ok but definitely not as good as my previous carrier. I knew almost immediately that when the contract was up, I was out! This past November it came time and I jumped to another carrier. They ported numbers over and told me to check with T-mobile to be sure that all lines shut down. December 19th I did just that, mind you it took me 3 hours in store to close because of some pin they said I set up in the initial start that 2 years later, I had never used. I spoke at that time to both in store associates and customer care and both assured me that I would only pay the balance they requested that day to complete the close of all lines. Neither would provide this so called “pin” even with my ID. I thought that would be the end, but a friend mentioned to me that they had something similar happen and their account never closed, causing another bill. Something told me with all the trouble I had with them to check on it. On Jan 29, I went in store to confirm that I was all set. Some how I had a bill larger than a normal monthly bill, I spent about an hour in there arguing with them before agreeing to pay another $288. They credited my account for an additional amount since they had seen that it was an error on their part. I verified several times that this was the last and final bill. Customer care and associates, again say that is correct and I reluctantly pay and leave.Mind you, once you port your numbers T-Mobile shuts down all access to your account and you cannot see or track any bills or payments. Well, it is now August… I receive a $175 bill from T-Mobile, but this time it comes from a bill collector! What?!? I go back in the store, they pull up all notes and agree that this bill should never have happened, call customer service and discuss my account. They have no explanation, but tell me it’s my problem, because it’s gone to collections. I will have to dispute the charge. They cannot help, customer care did not speak to me directly. I’m not gonna lye, I blew up in the store. To be clear, I NEVER received any bills. The store says they have an old address, but the debt collectors sure knew where to send it. I went home and called customer care myself.These people wouldn’t even talk to me about my account, because I don’t know the “pin” to my account. I have always used the same pins for everything, if I had a pin I know exactly what numbers it would be first of all, but no one in the company could or would talk to me about this account. Saying that they can see my account without the pin and don’t know where the charges came from??? Refusing to take care of or help in anyway! Again they told me I had to go to dispute with the debt collector and hung up in my face, when I told them that I am not dumb and know they can see all things related to my account. I called the debt collector to try to dispute it, and they told me that T-Mobile has to zero the debt out, in order to dispute and if I don’t pay it, I will see the hit to my credit. I AM PISSED AND WANT EVERYONE WHO DEALS WITH T-MOBILE TO BE AWARE….IF YOU READ THEIR REVIEWS THIS IS HAPPENING OVER AND OVER TO MULTIPLE PEOPLE. IF YOU GOOGLE IT ALSO SHOWS A CLASS ACTION LAWSUIT AGAINST T-MOBILE FOR WHAT APPEARS TO ALSO BE FRAUDULANT PRACTICES. PLEASE MOVE ACCORDINGLY IF YOU USE THEM FOR YOUR CARRIER. As for me, next step is either the news or a lawyer! 
Sorry for the long rant, I would hate for y’all to have to deal with this non-sense!

Purchased an upgrade on my phone through their online rep. The phone I wanted they did not have in the local store so I asked if they could ship it to the store and I pick it up there. I was told this would not be an issue so we completed the purchase and I paid taxes and activation fees. Received text and UPS tracking notification that the phone had arrived at the store and was ready for pickup. I get to the store location and was told they don’t have the phone but they can see the transaction on my account. My only option is to wait in limbo while they “do an investigation.” In the mean time the money I paid can’t be refunded and I can’t upgrade my phone or do anything until they complete their investigation. No one can tell me how long it will take or what their process involves. There is no one higher up I can speak with and nothing else they can do. I was told by the store sales person they usually don’t allow shipping to stores because this has happened before. If this is an issue why would it be an option at all. They know I don’t have the phone but someone signed for it at the store or a nearby business. Store claims the person who signed is not an employee there. I get the need to investigate but why hold me in limbo for who knows how long when they clearly know I don’t have the phone. Do better T-mobile!!
Very bad experience,always sometimes wrong.Internet connection is very bad.Customer service is like unreachable. Thinking about to leave TMobile after many years beeing a customer

This is a failure as company! T Mobile should be ashame to call there self a company.  They charge you for everything but you can't use any of it down to your insurance. The customerservice is a joke. They love to say they will call you back personally when they want you off the phone. Total failure to it's customer's

After 14 years as a customer and requesting an extension of 3 days until payday they shut off my service. I cannot call my doctor for medication or anything. This is ridiculous and petty on their part. I will be taking my phone to a competitor and switching service. My family and friends will also be switching as when you mess with one person sometimes it has a bigger ripple than you expected.

Run don't walk away from them service suks.  Tech guy messed up my phone and voicemails and the supervisor john via chat REFUSED to call me or have someone call me and I have black and white proof of this

Let me start by saying it is not the company but their employees. Stopped by the 14834 Pines Blvd Pembroke Pines, FL 33027 location yesterday. Phone broken and had no access. Was not sure if I had the insurance or not. Ask them what they can do for me. They responded with the option of getting a new phone and then told me I had to pay almost $300 on the spot. No problem, but I mentioned that I was online earlier, and it indicated that all I had to pay was $100 from my past due bill and $0 down. I asked about that, and the gentleman insisted that I had to pay the original amount he quoted. I said ok and not today. I stepped outside and called the company directly. Immediately they told me I had insurance and that I had the option of replacing the phone and waiting until next month to get the new phone that is scheduled to come out. WHY didn't the two gentlemen working in the store told me that? Is it because I am BLACK and my looks? I was coming from working outside so I must admit I looked a little out of place but I had my ID and verified my account. WTH!! I don't want to claim the race card, but it surely pointed to that!!

We canceled service and Tmobile continued to debit account. We called customer service continued to get runaround and no resolve to situation. We where a 20 year customer and over years watched bill go up service go down switched to Xfinty

Ridiculous if you are able to get a hold of somebody they'll will keep you waiting for 35+ minutes. After countless texts and phone calls no reply. I bet if I wanted to sign up for a new phone somebody would be available f****** joke!

Service is the best I've found, where i live.
(BUT)! Why is it everytime they download something to my phone for an update it screws my phone up?!?!?!?!?!
Right after DL some of my APPS don't work, I then have to REPLACE the App! I send a text message now and some kind of advertisement come up in the (D--)!!! TEXT!!! I would ask that no more DL' be sent to my phone, but then they would have some kind excuses as to why they do it, like it's a benefit to ME/AND I'M SURE THEY KNOW, WE KNOW ITS  (D--) LIE!!!
 Well if our Government does it then is OK, BUT NOT WITH ME!
I don't desire a text, or an email telling me how sorry you are for my troubles because, I CANT STAND BEING LIED TO!!!
Terrible experience the CSR show no empathy and are rude

-5 They owe us stars! I was lied to by a sales person. She asked if I wanted a watch. I said no. A little while into the "new phone upgrade" she asked again. Again I said NO. 3 more times she asked. I said I don't wear watches I will never again wear a watch and I certainly will never wear a computer on my body. She said, "Well it comes with the phone as a gift. It's free" I said no. She said people are using it as an insurance policy. I don't have to wear it but I can use it to locate my phone if I misplace it. She got me with logic and appealing my sense of security. SCAM ARTIST!  But it was free right?? Wrong!! She added a line $35 a month and $15 a month for the watch!! She never went over my bill even though I asked if my monthly charge was going to be the same - $83 a month. She said yes the same. THEN after a time of owning the phone I downloaded my pictures and PHOTOS OF PEOPLE I DON'T KNOW WERE ON MY PHONE!!!  IT IS A USED PHONE!! The store won't help, they won't connect me to the district manager, they don't believe when I say it's a used phone with glicthes and OTHER PEOPLE"S PICTURES. No one is getting back to me. And now THEY'RE COMPLAINING THAT I AM CALLING CUSTOMER CARE!!!!! Last response: "Well I can't guarantee that anyone is going to help you. They may or may not call back." YOU CAN'T GUARENTEE THAT ANYONE IS GOING TO DO THEIR JOB??? AND YOU'RE OK WITH SALES PEOPLE LYING??? STAY AWAY! IT IS A GERMAN OWNED COMPANY - LOOK IT UP.

I am 9 months pregnant. Less than two weeks from delivering. My husband and I needed to use this paycheck to get the last few baby items we needed before the baby arrives so even though we do not usually we decided to split our payment as T-Mobile allows. I wanted to pay half and the other half in two weeks. But T-Mobile only allowed for first payment in two weeks and the second in a month. Silly I thought, so I made the first payment (unknowingly doing so in an amount that was 1 cent off) through the app and intending to call T-Mobile later to cancel one of the auto pays. As I have done in the past. The customer service rep I got when I did call today, two weeks later was not only not helpful and rude but just kept telling me over and over again how this was my fault and she can't do anything. I made a fuss and she kept putting me on hold but resolved that if I made the minimum payment of $1.50 that would take care of the first payment and they could cancel the auto pay. This I was fine with and was so frustrated and crying I just wanted off the phone at this point. With the issue resolved we got off the phone and I took a much needed nap. The customer service rep continues calling and texting while I'm sleeping and I finally answer for her to tell me oh hey that didn't work and it will be charging you tomorrow, with zero recommendations and basically just telling me I'm screwed. T-Mobile is a freaking joke and I'll do nothing but tell everyone how awful your company is and I will be taking my business elsewhere as soon as possible. Great customer service not trying to solve anything and doing nothing but blaming the customer!

Terrible experience with T-Mobile's customers' service.
Paid the phone on May 1st, T-Mobile sent the wrong phone, returned the equipment,  has to pay again to get the correct phone.  The refund never came back after 3 months with numerous phone calls,  talking to over 20 different agents, every time they gave wrong story,  still fighting for the refund..

This is about the internet box that's supposed to be the best.(oakhillsCalifornia 92344)..it's not for 8 months I called about once every week to get it working and was replaced 4 times and about 19 techs but the service was never there heard every excuse...they said I will get a full refund but nothing

Always had really good service with them. Switched to AT&T and that was a joke. So went back to T-Mobile. I’m surprised to read all these bad reviews. The only I want to request from T-Mobile is that they have the same promo as Verizon. Ya know trade in any phone any condition and get the iPhone 15 on them. I rrrreally don’t wanna go with Verizon. But right now it seems that’s the only way I can get a new phone.

If I could give negative 5 I would. Their customer service is awful and what they tell you in the store is different than what they tell you over the phone or from one person to another. No consistency in processes and no idea how to be helpful at all.
Straight garbage. I got sos in my room. My ROOM. And the paying system is goofy

T mobile Prescott Pkwy lakes, my dog chewed up my phone,screen shot! Went to other t mobile.no could fix, phone repair shop $$$, Russ at lakes Pkwy said bring it in, I had little hope, 30 minutes later all done was able to recover all data save all my contacts photos etc etc.  Did the entire transfer.without a problem. He gets my vote!!!!!! C J

I wrote a review about the awful service I received at a Tmobile store. I gave them a 1 star rating. A "supposed " owner replied to the review with his email address. So I decided to take him up on his offer to discuss my review. Turns out that the email address was phony and probably the name of the owner as well! Tmobile is very helpful when you are signing up for a service. But when it's necessary to cancel the service, they aren't so helpful anymore! I had signed up for the 5g internet service to use at our campground park model. It didn't work very well and I took the modem device back to the Tmobile store I got it from to cancel the service. All of a sudden, I'm not allowed to return the device and they are not allowed to cancel the service either. I was told I had to call a number to cancel, they would email me a return label, then I had to box it up and take it to UPS within 14 days to return it. RIDICULOUS!!

T-Mobile is trash! If I could give them zero stars I would. Customer service is horrendous, rude and they over promise and do not deliver. They are not for the customer, they over charge us. I have had several times when my service was interrupted unnecessarily and added restore fees and they wouldn’t remove the fees but restored service and none of this was no fault of mine and all they could say is I apologize, this company is so un professional it’s ridiculous. I am very displeased and plan on leaving after years. I highly DO NOT recommend

When we set up our lines and bought our smart watches they accidentally charged us for an extra watch line. We unfortunately didn’t catch it and when we did they only refunded us 8 months out of 24 months. The person we spoke with would not transfer us to a manager and said that the manager said we would have to just eat the cost. He would not come to the phone. I find this ridiculous. 21 years with Sprint and we never had issues with service before. They get bought out by T Mobile and it’s been nothing but problems. Their home internet service is terrible.

If I could give them a (-5 star) I would. I dread being with this company and is seriously going to change companies as soon as I get a chance to. The way this economy is setup its already difficult for one person to manage paying their necessities like rent/mortgage, utilities etc, it makes no sense having to pay a phone bill that’s almost equal to the same amount as the electrical bill. This company rates are ridiculous and have no problem shutting you off. Knowing everyone needs a way to communicate, uses this to their advantage to rip people off.

I am deeply disappointed with the service provided by T-Mobile. We agreed to a two-week free trial on June 21, 2024, after two of their representatives visited our office. They set up the service on June 27, but it was not functional. They came back on July 1 to fix it, but within 20 minutes of their departure, our systems crashed due to issues with the T-Mobile network, causing us to lose access to our patient database.

Despite multiple attempts to get help from T-Mobile, we were met with unresponsive support. I had to manually troubleshoot the issue and revert to our original internet service. We wanted to cancel the trial, but were told we needed to visit a T-Mobile store. Due to the 4th of July weekend, we couldn’t get there until July 8, which was still within the trial period from the second setup.

Now, we are being billed for the service despite the numerous issues we encountered. Every attempt to resolve this by phone has resulted in being put on hold or transferred to incorrect departments. This level of customer service is unacceptable and has caused significant disruption to our operations.

I would not recommend T-Mobile based on this experience. The lack of effective support and resolution to our problem has been extremely frustrating.
Terrible service and customer services

July 5, 2024 I was going to switch to Verizon. I had such poor service with them that I cancelled the request to port my number over and use their service. That's when my trouble ramped up with T Mobile. Ever since attempting to switch he can receive texts messages from me but I can't get text messages from my spouse. We can call each other. . He called his carrier and they said they show that I have no service.  I get text messages from others and received them from others with no issue. I talked to the T Mobile tech people who couldn't resolve the issue. They said they would call me back. They never did. This was mid July. I logged into my account and they have also now completely messed up my billing. They said I am passed due and I have not been passed due with them ever. All I did was change my payment method. I talked to someone else today 7/28/2024 and they said they would fix my bill but it still looks messed up to me as it still looks like I am passed due. If you don't charge my payment method how can you say I am passed due? They are also billing me money beyond my usual monthly payments and I don't know what for and it could not be explained to me. I have been with T Mobile for over 20 years and the whole reason I tried to switch in the first place is because I didn't want to wait in line for a phone for three hours. I want my bill corrected, I want my current payment method to be used to pay my bill, and I want my phone fixed. I would also like a free new phone after 20 years and all of this aggravation I deserve it.

T-Mobile is the worst service, I literally got a phone call because I left a penny off of my payment.  I woke up to my phone being cut off.  The customer service is horrible and they can never seem to help with anything.  I am seeking another phone carrier.  TMobile is a scam.

I switched to T-Mobile (July 2024), and what a mistake.  I was sold on several promotions to include free Hulu.  I was told by the sales person that I could add to my Hulu subscription (after activation), additional subscriptions such as Disney and ESPN+, only later to find out that I can't add any additional subscriptions without giving up my free Hulu.  During the switch over to T-Mobile, one of the phones could not make cellular calls.  In speaking with Technical support they told me the phone had to be taken to a T-Mobile store.  We did, and they were able to get the phone working, but we were also told the fix could have been done over the phone with Technical support.  Further, my first bill did not include any of the discounts associated with my service plan.  Buyer beware.  If I had it to do over again I would never had made the switch to T-Mobile.

T-mobile does false advertising. They had a promotional offer for their home internet, that if you add a t-mobile home internet, you’d receive a 50 inch 4k Amazon TV, valued at $450. I added t-mobile home internet, applied for the promotion, and was approved to receive the promotional offer, however, t-mobile did not send me the tv. After over 23 hours of phone conversations with different departments of t-mobile’s customer service, the t-mobile agent only offered a $260 credit toward my account. I refused the $260 and asked for the tv that I was offered or the $450 value. And t-mobile refused my request. This is false advertising and t-mobile should know better and not treat their customers this way. They should honor what they promised, either the tv or the value of the promotion.

I was a T-Mobile customer for about three months for my business.  When I signed up, I was promised rebates for bringing my own device and for switching from a competitor.  Understandably, there was a 90 day wait for those rebates.  However, the rep also promised me a $100 credit per month for four months to cover a Comcast Mobile termination fee.  I had this in writing from the T-Mobile sales rep.  I had to call each month for over one hour each time and talk to various reps to get the credit.  It was a fight every month.  In addition, I live in Delaware near the beach and the reception was horrible.  Dropped calls frequently (including when I was calling T-Mobile customer service).  Yes, the service reps are very nice but the bottom line was the never did what they said they were going to do and the overall network service in both Maryland and Delaware was marginal at best (on all the lines I had).  When I finally dropped T-Mobile I was four days into the next billing cycle.  They charge for the for the full month and do not pro rate.  It was a very bad experience dealing with the network quality and literally hours on the phone with them trying to settle billing issues.

I used to love T-Mobile.  They, in my experience, still have the best international plan of any phone/data company.  They were so good that I recently added a friend to my account.  Here is the rub:  It was a total debacle. The sales rep over promised and overwhelmingly under delivered. My friend and I tried everything, from calling, to emailing, to facebook messaging, to contacting corporate to untangle the mess.  After a month, they have over-billed me and not followed up on numerous promises.  I've now dedicated 30+ hours and put my friendship in jeopardy...all due to T-Mobile's incompetence.  Their mantra:  A communications company who can't communicate.  What a disgraceful example of corporate America.
They kept telling me my bill was going to be one thing but when I got the bill it was much higher

Slow data outside bigger cities.
Verizon is a better mobile carrier in US.

Worst customer service of any company ever with the exception of PayPal ! I don't get my text from the Braves or PayPal every other text I get. I miss the first five innings of the Braves game because you have to have a smartphone to get in the gate they don't have paper tickets anymore. And this T-Mobile phone couldn't get text from the Braves for some idiotic reason. So T-Mobile ruined my birthday I'm canceling my service with them and those incompetent morons will never get another cent from me ! And they almost got me evicted when I couldn't get a text code to confirm my identity for 3 months now. Stay away from T-Mobile and PayPal or you could get a nervous breakdown like me. Cried and yelled at them for hours now the last three days and this is the third time their incompetence has caused me great misery ! Phug T-Mobile 💩

When I tell you this has been the worst experience and I've been with them for 8 years!  Their reps need to be brought back yo the US and retrained!  I have taken over my mom's plan years ago were speaking 8 years ago... On yhe phone told them etc.  I'm not even going to explain the crazy they took me through today but the rep keeps telling me they need to speak with my 87 year old Mom that has demitia! I told them I have POA days ago... But he keeps asking to speak with her...  Whyyyyy! I offered to send it over !  Ridiculous!

Do NOT use T mobile.   We were a Sprint customer for over 20 years.  They switched to T mobile and everything changed especially the customer service.  We were given a third line for free.  When we switched companies, they wouldn’t let us take the line number with us for the third line so we canceled the number.   They charged us almost $300 to cancel the free line.  I fought it but they said it was a valid charge.  How???   We weren’t under a contract and the line was free.  Absolutely garbage.  Everyone I talked to was clueless and rude.

Was told my credit card would be refunded. When I called back I was told the refund was denied. After I expressed my anger the agent spoke to her supervisor and I was told I would receive an email the next day stating I would be receiving a refund. I never received an email or a refund as of today. I am still chasing the refund for the principal of it.

I went to the T-Mobile store on Power and McKellips in Mesa, AZ. My son and I went in to buy new iPhones. We were told that we could get 2 iPhone 14’s for free since we had 4 lines. That wasn’t what we wanted.. but they convinced us to get them. Then when my phone was finishing setting up and the glass was already on.. another employee told us that we had been overcharged for the glass accessory. Last night I was watching TV, and a T-Mobile commercial came on. They said that the deal was 2 iPhone 15’s for free with 4 lines. I was furious!! I had been lied to and cheated. I never had these issues with Sprint. This will make me not trust people who sell me things. I am furious and will tell everyone who will listen. T-Mobile stinks!!

T-Mobile customer care, suck big time, the peoples don't know what their ate doing, don't care what problem you are having don't have the knowledge to resolves your problem Everytime I call get the same thing my call gets disconnected today it happen to me 2 times, I was left again with no
Worst company ever. I made a new deal with them with agreement,  once I changed from my previous company to them , they changed everything.  Don't trust them.

For several months, my bill has been incorrect.  Each biilling cycle I must go into the store and have it corrected,  The store manager calls the company offices and says they fixed it.  Just got another incorrect bill. Maybe they should let me move in since I am there so often  Follow up to above - upper management says the store was wrong, and my bill is $26 more per month than what I was quoted! All I’m going to state is STAY AWAY FROM THIS HORRIBLE COMPANY . 
Do not waste your money or time on this company that’s the most transparent warning ⚠️ I can provide. DO NOT PURCHASE T-Mobile service. Save yourself now.

Their service socks. I'm a prior customer of Sprint and since they took over the service has went down. I have had to call them every day this week about me not being able to have a conversation with people cause my lines keep breaking u or they can't hear me at all. Still nothing is being done. Sending a new phone, but it's not the phone, it's in their towers. Told me at first they were working on the towers, then they had completed the work, but it's still happening. I tried a phone with AT&T service and it works perfect. Been with them over 20 years. Bout time to find another provider.

I joined T-mobile when it was the "uncarrier" under Jonh Legere.  The service wasn't always great, but they tried, and I wanted to support that.  We were on a family plan, and after about 15 years, and as the kids aged, it made sense for us to switch to a different carrier that better fit our needs.  That's when the nightmare started.

I got an letter saying that I never returned a 4G range extender that I received in 2012.  Yes, 4G and 2012.  It had never worked for us, so we returned it to the store at least 7 years ago.   There was no indication on our account that T-Mobile still thought we had it, so I had long since lost any receipt -- it was off the account, so why hold it?

So I called T-mobile at least 10 times over 7 months.  First they said they were trying to locate it, then said I'd receive a credit since they found it.  The credit hadn't been issued, so I called again, at which time they said that since they had no record of it, I owed the full original price, plus a late fee, and there was no way to appeal.  Remember: this was a 4G (which is seldom used) device that was over 12 years old and they told me that they would issue a credit ... until today.  Oh, and if I didn't pay, they'd ding my credit rating.

So today I paid over $200 to be done forever with T-mobile.  I hope other people have better experiences, and I loved "old"  T-mobile, but I couldn't recommend T-mobile of today.  To say for months that I was all set, then to turn on a dime -- for a device with no value that they should have known I returned -- is (IMO) terrible service, so to anyone who asks, I'd recommend trying another carrier.

I paid off all 4 of my lines early. All I wanted was proof. I changed carriers because I didn't have service at my new office.  I just needed them to email me the payoff proof. I was told they couldn't due to security and privacy. But they can send me all the other information on my account. Literally everything but what I need.  Why is this so hard. I was hangup on and I am not beyond reasoning.. I was told to use the app if I still had it.  As if I hadn't already tried to get in several times but once I ported the  numbers out they closed the account. You can't use the app once they close it. As if they didn't know that already. I can buy a phone but I can't access my account that I hadn't canceled yet because I needed this information. If I ever thought about going back there is no way I would ever now..  awful!!

I had a frustrating experience with T-Mobile earlier this year. The service representative provided a lot of misinformation and didn't represent the T-Mobile brand well at all. I was misled about qualifying for the $300 gift card, which I never received. Additionally, I was billed $6.26 for an invalid charge, which was the main reason I decided to switch my service back to Spectrum. When I called customer service to resolve the bill, the representative claimed he needed manager approval but didn't offer any real assistance. Overall, my experience was disappointing, and I hope T-Mobile improves its customer service.

Ever since I've had T-Mobile, it's been a joak. Today was icing on the 28th of June for the month of July end. Store then on the 18th I wake up to my phone and my internet being turned off saying that I needed to pay $45.06. I had to pay that cause I needed the phone.  And when I paid it they're telling me they're gonna charge me in additional Twenty dollars per line that got disconnected I would not recommended them to my worst enemy there always fees every month it is a new fee and won't happen again I am going to pay my phones off and change my services if You're thinking about switching to t mobile DON'T DO IT learn from my mistake

Recently switched my service from Verizon to T-Mobile. I called & got prices on phones / plans, etc. I asked specific questions & made very notes on what I was told. 3 phones were supposed to be $0 per month after credit (get an Iphone on us!) Asked several times, so..what is the monthly credit on each bill? $34.59 each month for 3 dif lines for a total due of $0; one other line was supposed to be credited at $16.67 per month for a total due of $17.92. Basically a total of $17.92 due for 4 lines. When the bill comes due, its actually $47.51 for 4 lines of equipment. Im told no one can fix it. I have made numerous calls, talked to a 'supervisor' and 'Ass. Coach' and still told no one can fix it. Now they have my trade-in phones that were all good and paid for.

My wife and I needed to upgrade our phones and service to 5G. We had stopped two weeks before at the Fairlawn, Ohio store. I had a minor medical procedure and my wife wanted to look at phones. The representative James wanted us to try the home internet. In our area it doesn't work so I took it back. My wife and I live of SSI, so we waited until payday. We knew about the $35 activation fee per phone. We had not been told we would have to pay $20 apiece for the charger. James tried to sell us cases at $30 each and $20 for screen protectors. REALLY? We didn't bite on that. Our old phone values were $150 each trade in.The new phones were $400. We were taxed on the full value of $910. This process was NOT explained to us. My phone will stay active until one of us dies. As far as technology,  TMOBILE is great. Customer service 0.

This company's software will flag your account. Lock your card, keep you from accessing your money and they won't do anything about it. They'll refresh your card and you have to wait 24 hours to access your money. This place is c*** I would not recommend it to anybody.

We signed up and paid the tax on the phones and router.  Bryan Johnson said he's the main boss of the business unit out of Nashville Tennessee and promised us he would personally handle our getting activated.  He failed to ever answer us back.
Then after 5 days we recieved a month's bill for $206.00.  Keep in mind we still don't have internet or use of our new phones and still recieved a bill anyway.
After complaining to countless other so called specialists we were told we still have to pay our month's bill even know we sent back all phones and router and never received service.
What a bunch of Losers, especially Bryan Johnson!!!

I bought a phone directly from Samsung that was unlocked. You could choose a carrier you wanted so I went with T-Mobile. The phone cost $1104. I decided to change carriers. The phone and the T-Mobile account are completely paid for. The phone however is locked to T-Mobile and they don't know how to unlock it. I am stuck with an $1100 phone that is useless. Samsung says it's T-Mobile. T-Mobile says it's Samsung. Who is responsible?

Incompetent help
Beginning Feb 12th I reported that my line was not receiving one time passwords (OTP) and could they add code or whatever was missing.  They never fixed it (I would have gone to another carrier but they had the best signal in the neighborhood. This OTP issue was reported to them about 20 times with their same nonsense and they put in a service ticket and nothing was wrong on their end, even went to T-M store who checked with service and no one can help.  On May 14 I chatted with Felicia who had the problem fixed since T-M set up the line incorrectly- I told them that on Feb 12th!
I am updating due to the Sept 2024 experience. I received an email from them (T-M) saying my service was suspended for non-payment.  No line or messages were working- Since I was auto-pay, I checked my credit card and saw that I did pay T-M.  Called and the automated system immediately said did I want to pay and restore service. Assuming that the "extra"  payment would be in my account and I could restore service quickly, I paid again.  Still no service.  Finally spoke to someone who could see that I paid twice.  Took them 24 hours to restore the line.  Messages were still blocked by T-M for 7 days!!! until they fully restored service.  They gave me a credit but I would have preferred to have service.  These issues should not be a surprise to the CEO if he is doing his job!

My boyfriend had just switched to T Mobile while he was cleaning up flood damage in MN and had destroyed his previous phone in the water. We were told we could drive to a store to pay his bill as he  did not want to be late. There were a total of 5 people working at this location. We were aware that a couple of them were training, however no one in the store really had a solid idea on how to handle a simple transaction. They had originally told us that we could make the payment no problem, then were informed that they would not accept a check so we said okay, we can pay cash. Then we were told they could do an e check but that also turned out to be impossible somehow. When it was decided that we would just pay cash, the employee informed us that it would be an extra $5.00 charge to pay in store! That is horrible customer service. We made sure to drive to a location to pay the bill to your company so it would not be late and we are going to be punished for that? For a new customer, or any customer for that matter, it does not show much confidence in the company.

I signed up for a deal that they take automatically from my checking account. Not the credit card but checking account. That should have been the red flag for me. But I went ahead with this. The deal was that I pay for their service and for the phone. At the end of 24 months period the phone was supposed to be unlocked and mine. The service was bad, the technical support was unhelpful, their customer service rude and useless. Of course, no surprise that I wanted to leave at the end of this 24 month period. It was a full time job to get my account number and a transfer code from them Finally I got that and move on to another carrier. But they locked my phone and it's been several days I am trying them to get it unlock and they blame Samsung, Florida, heat anything in the world but not unlocking the phone . They have no legal right to keep it but they know that take them to court is a hassle so they steal. This company has nothing to offer. They are just thieves!

I recently had a highly troubling experience with T-Mobile that left me deeply dissatisfied. Somehow, someone managed to gain access to my account. It started with a call from a person impersonating a T-Mobile employee from the loyalty department. They knew all my personal details, including how long I've been with T-Mobile, how many lines I have, my exact monthly bill, and more. They promised me a discount on my bills and even deposited money into my T-Mobile account to appear legitimate.

The impersonator offered me two free iPhone 15 1TB devices, but instead, I received iPhone 15s with only 500MB. They tracked the delivery and knew exactly when the phones would arrive. After realizing the mistake, they called to apologize and provided a return address to send the phones back. Everything happened so quickly that it felt like a whirlwind.

When I reported the incident to T-Mobile, I expected them to take swift action to resolve the issue. Instead, they informed me that since the phones were delivered to my house and someone in my home signed for them, they are not responsible. Despite my detailed report, T-Mobile refuses to take accountability, leaving me to bear the cost of the phones.

This experience has been incredibly frustrating and has shaken my trust in T-Mobile's security measures. Their lack of support in resolving this matter is deeply disappointing, and I am now stuck with a significant financial burden due to their inadequate handling of the situation.

T-Mobile is like  scam. When I sign up with them at Costco, they Promise me with all that rebate and promotion And the plans I need to sign up. Note that they never sent me a bill for these months of charges. I used no service and had no device of theirs! So she promises to refund me. Instead she makes the mistake of double charging me for all months of service that were supposed to be credited!! How fill in the blank can a person get? So I make the mistake of calling yet again (5 hrs now!!) and finally get someone who claims to be a supervisor. Wants to blame the whole thing on me! Tell me how to talk and not so loudly!!! Yes she too promised to remove the charges and the double charges and also errs and triple charges me!! Totally insane. The company has turned into a scam. Full on scam! God help the folks who are employed to rip us off and have no other way to make a living. You might also ask why after 15 years would I ditch Tmobile and go to Visible? Tmobile started charging me an extra $10 per month, because they billed my credit card not my bank directly. No way was I going to give these fill in the blank my bank account no!!! I had always used a credit card and was charged in advance each month. Nowhere in my agreement did it say they could charge $10 for using a credit card. They claim that banks started charging them higher fees!!! Liars!! Banks are not charging them more than 1.75% For my bill that would have been about $1.20 and banks were charging that all along. Some greedy fill in the blank at Tmobile thought it would be a good idea to steal another $9 from senior citizens. I began raising disputes on the $10 charge and calling out the breach of contract. They kept up the charges so I found a better deal at Visible. Now Tmobile is worse than Sprint was. Its become the telecom sewer. Tmobile cons and steals then hire impoverished uneducated people with attitudes in a 3rd world country to terrorize you. This is the criminal state of Telecom today. Yes I filed complaints with the FCC, my attorney general, and bank. I will sue if necessary!!!

THIS COMPANY SAID T-MOBILE HOME  INTERNET PER MONTH ONLY $60,,,, BUT THEY CHARGED ME $240/MONTH EVEN I USED ONLY 3 DAYS... ESTABLISED TO LOOT MONEY FROM CUSTOMER...

I wish I would've known that trading in my iphone would result in a 2-month long headache, involving 8 customer service encounters with the store and online reps. I'm convinced T-mobile has a plan to bait and switch you, then stall until your 90-day return period expires. "Trade in your old phone, extend your contact for 2 years at no additional cost to upgrade to a new phone, and keep the same plan." Then your bill arrives with a $15 monthly installment fee, NOT disclosed at the time of purchase at the store. It's not in the contract either... which I was oddly not provided with, neither print nor email. Since this event on April 19, I have been promised a call back and an emailed copy of the corrected contract on May 21, May 23, again from a supervisor on May 23, June 1, June 6, again from a supervisor on June 6... see my screenshots of online customer service chat--they blow smoke and run down the clock on your 90 days, doing nothing to solve the problem. Today I called my original T-Mobile store on Rainbow & 215 to request help from a manager. The rep refused to forward my call to a supervisor, refused to provide an email address for a supervisor, and for the 8th time, said I would get a call back from someone in 2-3 days. If they actually call me back and resolve the issue, I'll update my review, but otherwise be warned that T-Mobile is totally fine with treating their longtime customers like garbage.

I am a new customer with 2 year locked price deal. I have had to update my credit card on file and have not been able to get anyone to connect with me. I sent a chat and received and email response but no one ever checked back with me. I just saw that my phone has been disconnected and now they want to charge me to turn it back on! I have never struggled so hard to give someone my money!
Terrible customer service!

I have been a T-Mobile customer for many many years. I was recently offered an upgrade by 2 different sales people. 3 lines for $165 and free iphones. Received my phones and the first bill. I'm being charges monthly for each phone. After many phone calls to customer service i'm being told that they would never offer a free phone. Then they promised to provide a 30$ monthly credit for phone charge, but that was a lie too. That will never happen. All i hear is that i'm sorry. We made a mistake. Don't ever buy T-mobile service. They steal your money
Very dishonest company they do not do what they say overcharging our bill.wish I would have stayed with AT&T will switch when I can get out of contract

I have made atleast 5 long calls. In desperation stopped at different stores at least 5 times and no help getting my visual voice mail. The people on the help line just repeat same stuff everytime that does not work. On 2nd call right now for today supposedly getting manager ...still on hold.. if this call sucks like the rest then I'm going across street to at &t..okay call just hung up. I'm done .

Terrible customer service, but what would you expect from a dishonest company. Every month when I look at my bill, I have to spend an hour or more on the phone with Tmobile trying to get some sneaky additional fees removed. After wasting time trying to upsell me to new services, they inevitably admit that the charges were added in error, but next month it happens again. I think they just keep trying, believing that eventually they can pass one by me without my noticing. These fees range from $50 to over $100 per month. Agents are polite but feel free to lie as necessary to make the customer happy. Just because you call in and get your complaint "resolved" does not mean that you are not going to have to call in again when you find that your bill is still incorrect and they have charged your card. Then be prepared to waste more time with agents who assume you are in the wrong and fail to grasp the problem. An additional trick they use is to charge you the erroneous fees but say that they will reduce your next month's bill by the amount in question. However on the next bill, they will have made the adjustment but added some other false fees and random deductions to create lots of confusion which is far beyond any of their support team's capability of understanding. I suppose they are banking on customers just giving up. Frustrating!

I have been with T-Mobile for seven years. Part of that was with sprint, and the service has always exceeded my expectations. The employees are well-versed on all the devices, including my iPhone and iPad and T-Mobile Internet. I shop at Sams Club in Grand Prairie and find that there T-Mobile set up is very convenient very friendly and helpful. I am working with GG and James Jordan (upgrading my devices)who’s always been very professional and friendly. I like going to these Satellite T-Mobile spots as they can answer all my questions in a timely matter and I don’t have to wait in line like I do at some of the T-Mobile stores and if there is a line, I can always do my shopping and when I come back, they are ready to answer my questions. The main reason I’m sticking with T-Mobile is there pricing and plans ….and I  know that the people that work at T-Mobile at Sams Club know their stuff and take care of me as well as hundreds of other customers and  BUSINESS Customers that shopped there on the daily basis.
                  AJ SNACKS
